stories.title: Charles Schwab Challenge Final Round — Eric Cole Chases First PGA Tour Win

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
FINAL ROUND underway at Colonial Country Club.

Eric Cole leads at -12, Ryan Gerard one back at -11, Mac Meissner at -10.

The final group has ONE PGA Tour victory combined. Cole chasing his FIRST.

Matsuyama (-9) and Åberg (-7) lurk. All to play for. ⛳️ #CharlesSchwabChallenge

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Colonial R4 leaderboard check:

Cole/Gerard/Meissner are playing for a title most didn't see coming.

Fowler and Finau missed the cut.

Åberg (new putter, -7) is still looking for win No. 2.

This is Hogan's Alley. ⛳️ #CharlesSchwabChallenge

facebook.long_form:
Colonial Country Club is ready for its final act. The Charles Schwab Challenge enters its concluding round today at one of the most storied venues in professional golf — and the leaderboard looks nothing like most people predicted when the week began.

Eric Cole leads the way at 12 under par, following a stunning seven-under 63 on Saturday that included eight birdies and just one bogey. Right behind him is Ryan Gerard at 11 under, who closed Saturday with back-to-back birdies to secure his spot in the final group. Mac Meissner rounds out the final pairing at 10 under.

Here's the remarkable thing about Sunday's final group: between Cole, Gerard, and Meissner, they own a combined total of one PGA Tour victory. Cole is still searching for his first, as is Meissner. This is Hogan's Alley — a course that rewards precision and course management — and the leaderboard reflects the grinding nature of what it asks.

Hideki Matsuyama is lurking at nine under, poised to pounce if the leaders stumble. Ludvig Åberg, who switched putters entering the week, is at seven under in a tie for 12th — closer than expected, given how the week started, but still needing a low round to get into the conversation.

The week had its notable casualties too. Tony Finau shot back-to-back 70s to miss the cut at even par. Rickie Fowler, who had been showing recent form, opened with a 70 and stumbled to a 73 on Friday to miss the cut as well.

Sunday at Colonial begins at 12:55 PM EDT for the final group, live on Golf Channel and ESPN+. Watch closely — first-time winners make history at tracks like this one.
facebook.image_caption: Cole leads. Gerard and Meissner one back. One PGA Tour win in the final group. This is what makes golf great. ⛳️ #CharlesSchwabChallenge #PGATour

article.headline: Eric Cole Leads Schwab Challenge Final Round at -12, Chasing First PGA Tour Win

stories.title: US Women's Open Preview — Korda Targeting a 4th 2026 Title at Historic Riviera

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The US Women's Open arrives at Riviera CC in 4 days.

First time USGA women's golf plays Riviera — a major course for a major championship.

Korda is the favorite. Thitikul is World No. 1. Stark defends.

June 4-7. Don't miss it. 🏆 #USWomensOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda in 2026: 3 wins. 3 runner-ups. 6 starts.

She has not finished outside the top 2 all year.

The last person to do that? Annika Sorenstam in 2001.

She's heading to the US Women's Open at Riviera as the favorite. #USWomensOpen #Korda

facebook.long_form:
One of the most anticipated women's majors in recent memory is four days away.

The 81st U.S. Women's Open Presented by Ally tees off at Riviera Country Club in Pacific Palisades, California on June 4 — and it marks the first time in USGA history that a women's major championship has been played at Riviera. The course known as "The Riviera of the West" — a venue that hosts men's PGA Tour events every year — will now test the best women's golfers in the world.

And those golfers know who they're chasing.

Nelly Korda arrives at Riviera as the overwhelming tournament favorite, following one of the most dominant starts to an LPGA season in decades. In six starts in 2026, she has won three times and finished runner-up in the other three. She won the Chevron Championship for her third major title. The week after, she won the Riviera Maya Open at Mayakoba by four shots for her 18th career LPGA title — making her the youngest American to reach that number since Nancy Lopez. She has not finished outside the top two in any event this year, matching a feat previously achieved only by Annika Sorenstam in 2001.

World No. 1 Atthaya "Jeeno" Thitikul will look to challenge, as will defending champion Maja Stark, who returns to defend her title. Eleven past US Women's Open champions are in the field, and all 25 of the world's top-ranked players have entered.

Riviera is a course where precision matters more than distance, and course management rewards experience. Whether Korda can transfer her current dominance to a major venue she hasn't won at before will be the central question of the week.

US Women's Open: June 4-7 at Riviera CC. Don't miss it.
facebook.image_caption: Riviera. June 4-7. The first USGA women's major at this iconic venue. Korda enters as the favorite. Thitikul, Stark, and the world's best await. 🏆 #USWomensOpen #LPGA

article.headline: US Women's Open 2026: Riviera Hosts Its First USGA Women's Major, With Korda as the Runaway Favorite

stories.title: LIV Golf Korea Final Round — Niemann and Gooch Co-Lead at Asiad CC

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Final round at LIV Golf Korea in Busan.

Niemann and Gooch co-lead at -9. Crushers GC lead the team standings.

DeChambeau lurks. This one could come down to the wire.

Asiad CC. Final round today. #LIVGolf #LIVGolfKorea

facebook.long_form:
South Korea's debut on the LIV Golf calendar reaches its conclusion today.

At the Asiad Country Club in Busan, Joaquin Niemann and Talor Gooch stand tied at nine under par as the final round gets underway. Both have proven themselves as LIV Golf champions before — Niemann is the Chilean fan favorite and one of the most talented ballstrikers in the game, while Gooch, the former FedEx Cup champion, has demonstrated a cold efficiency under pressure throughout his LIV career.

Niemann's team, the Crushers GC, leads the team leaderboard, giving the individual and team competitions compelling stakes simultaneously. Bryson DeChambeau, who shared the Round 1 lead, remains in the picture and will be pushing both leaders hard in the final round.

Whoever wins at Asiad CC today takes their place in LIV Golf history as the first champion at what promises to be a returning destination for the tour. The final round is underway — check live scores at the official LIV Golf website.
facebook.image_caption: Niemann and Gooch. Tied. Final round in Busan. Who wins LIV Golf Korea? 🏌️ #LIVGolf #LIVGolfKorea

article.headline: LIV Golf Korea Final Round: Niemann and Gooch Co-Lead in Busan's Inaugural LIV Event

stories.title: Nelly Korda's Historic 2026 Season — Sorenstam Comparisons Are Earned

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Since January 1, Nelly Korda has played 6 LPGA events.

She has won 3. Finished runner-up in the other 3.

That's 18 career LPGA titles — the youngest American to reach that mark since Nancy Lopez.

The US Women's Open is next. The Sorenstam comparisons are real. #LPGA #Korda

facebook.long_form:
Numbers don't lie. And Nelly Korda's 2026 numbers are telling one of the most remarkable stories in women's golf history.

In six LPGA Tour starts this year, she has won three times and finished runner-up in the remaining three. Every single time she has teed it up, she has finished first or second. The last player to achieve that across an entire calendar year was Annika Sorenstam in 2001 — one of the greatest women's golfers of all time at the absolute peak of her dominance.

Her 2026 wins include the Chevron Championship, giving her three career major titles. She followed that with a four-shot victory at the Riviera Maya Open at Mayakoba for her 18th career LPGA title — making her the youngest American to reach 18 career wins since Nancy Lopez. The streak has included a third win in the 2026 calendar year that continued her extraordinary run.

Now she heads to the U.S. Women's Open at Riviera Country Club (June 4-7), where all 25 of the world's top-ranked players are entered and defending champion Maja Stark awaits. World No. 1 Atthaya Thitikul is the only player who could reasonably be described as a peer threat at this level right now.

If Korda wins at Riviera, the Sorenstam comparisons won't just be permitted — they'll be required.
facebook.image_caption: 3 wins. 3 runners-up. 6 starts. Nelly Korda has not finished outside the top 2 in 2026. Sorenstam did it. Korda is doing it. 🏆 #LPGA #Korda #USWomensOpen

article.headline: Nelly Korda's 2026 Season Is Historically Dominant — and the Sorenstam Comparisons Are Fully Earned

stories.title: Åberg's Schwab Challenge Putter Switch — Win Drought Continues Despite Changes

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Åberg switches putters at Colonial.

T12 at -7 through R3. Still no win since Genesis.

He has the talent. He's just missing the final close.

Fowler and Finau also missed the cut. ⛳️ #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

facebook.long_form:
Ludvig Åberg is one of the most talented young players in professional golf. He hits it long, he hits it straight, he sees the game with the kind of clarity that coaches describe when they talk about "seeing the course." And yet, heading into Sunday at Colonial, he sits tied for 12th at seven under par — searching, yet again, for the win that will move him from "promising" to "proven."

He arrived at the Charles Schwab Challenge having made a change: a new putter. After multiple top-10 finishes in 2026 without converting, Åberg decided the flatstick needed an upgrade. Through three rounds, the switch showed some results — he's made enough putts to stay in the conversation at Colonial. But being tied for 12th at the 54-hole mark doesn't put a trophy in his hands.

For context, Åberg won the Genesis Invitational earlier in 2026, showing that the win is in there. But converting the kind of sustained top-10 form he's shown into more victories has been the challenge.

The cut line also told a story this week: Tony Finau (even par, back-to-back 70s) and Rickie Fowler (70-73) both missed the weekend. Colonial can be a course that rewards ball-strikers who can scramble — and both found themselves without the form to capitalize.

The 2026 PGA Tour season is providing exactly the kind of wide-open competition that makes the sport compelling. Sunday at Colonial will produce another winner — and whoever it is will have earned it.
facebook.image_caption: New putter. T12 at -7. Still no win for Åberg since Genesis. Fowler and Finau missed the cut. Sunday at Colonial will produce someone's moment. ⛳️ #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

article.headline: Åberg's New Putter, a T12 at Colonial, and a Win Drought That Keeps Going
